title: Hiyori and Yasuie Reveal Wano’s Pain
description: On pages 6–9, this part focuses on Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #7, Kid, Yasuie, Unknown person #8 as Hiyori’s identity and Yasuie’s sacrifice pull the Wano rebellion into a deeper emotional crisis.
scene: Zoro protects Toko and meets Hiyori, while Yasuie prepares a sacrifice meant to rescue the rebellion’s plan.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Be sure to tell Kinemon and the others, too!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: We'll fight alongside y'all!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Things are going to get busy now! After all,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Most of us might not make it through the week left before the final battle without dropping dead! Ahaha!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: And now I'm off!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I am not sure if this is a

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Yo Yo!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: A hand- some man, a beautiful lady,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And the town girl! I'm on my way out!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: So...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even after listening to what that old man said, I still don't know exactly how he's related to you all.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Same here.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Yes. He certainly is a very cheery man, that tonyasu. Who is he?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: thing is...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I don't really...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: We don't know a either.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: WhAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even though you didn't know, you just let him talk on and on like that?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Er, well, because it was so one- sided,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: And he was addressing us so casually,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How could anyone forget a guy like that?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And because he might have been some important person in the past, asking would be rude, so...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I suppose because he's easygoing...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He might be a childhood friend? Or a teacher or elder student from your lessons?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Nnnhhh! Who is he?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: What AAA?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Gramps, Gramps, How's Your Nausea?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Oh, Tonyasu.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Lying in bed sure sucks!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Sucks more than working, I'll tell ya!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Look, I'll leave some millet and water here,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Try to eat, alright?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: AHAHAHAHAHAHAHA... you really are such a good person.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: "Yesu, I don't have much time left, so... you should go ahead and eat whatever you brought.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Yoyo! Why, you're quite the silver-tongued heartstealer!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll give ya two extra grains of millet for that!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oi, Seaweed Granny! Do you have enough medicine?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: No, I don't but just seeing your face helps me feel a little better.

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.text: IYOOO! You man- killer, you!()

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Mr. Retiree! How's the knee?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Oh! Yesu!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: O-Saki! How's the baby?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Hogya!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Hanko! How's Mom's sickness?

dialogue.speaker: Yasuie
dialogue.text: Yasuie! Let's play!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Here ya go. It's not much, but you should eat up.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Looks like Tonyasu's got some extra cash.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I didn't think he'd be going around giving every- one food.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: He doesn't. Labor wages are basically zip.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He had to sell his only daughter, TOko,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: into the capital. She works there now.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: She sends back whatever she makes to Yasuie.

dialogue.speaker: Toko
dialogue.text: TOKO...

dialogue.speaker: Toko
dialogue.text: But I'm not a man! Ahaha!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: H- Her?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: come to think of it, she smiled all the time like a crazy person, too.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: because of all the trouble toko goes through to send it to him,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He feels bad keeping it all to himself. He spreads it around to people that can't work. That's how he spends his days.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Regardless of whether or not he gets to eat.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: his face and body don't look the part, but

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That man's been loved by everyone here for as long as I can remember.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: He's their Buddha.

summary: This section advances the Wano - Hiyori and Yasuie storyline with 62 dialogue beats. Hiyori and Yasuie bring the rebellion’s emotional history into the foreground.
